methods.description: This method involves analyzing the Schrödinger equation using the generalized Morse potential (GMP) to describe the energy spectrum of diatomic molecules and their electromagnetic transitions. The method includes transforming the equation into dimensionless variables and solving it using a confluent hypergeometric function.

steps.description: Make the change of variable x = ar to obtain a dynamic equation with dimensionless variables.
steps.searchable_summary: transform Schrödinger equation to dimensionless variables

steps.description: Introduce a new variable y and a function F(y) to define the equation in a solvable form.
steps.searchable_summary: define new variable and function for equation transformation

steps.description: Solve the transformed equation using a confluent hypergeometric function.
steps.searchable_summary: solve equation using confluent hypergeometric function

steps.description: Express each quantum number n in terms of η, ensuring the energy spectrum is finite.
steps.searchable_summary: relate quantum numbers to system energy spectrum
